description: Generate a diceware passphrase from the EFF long wordlist (7776 words, ~12.9 bits/word).

platform.value_on_windows: Windows ships no secure generator out of the box; the common Get-Random idiom is non-cryptographic. mksecret is secure-by-default with no PowerShell-version dependency.
platform.value_on_unix: One self-contained binary covering passwords, diceware passphrases (missing on every OS), and encoded keys — secure-by-default (no pwgen -s footgun, no Python runtime for diceware).

prefer_default_when: hashing or signing data — use digest

options.description: Structured JSON metadata for AI agents
Capitalise the first letter of each word.
Append a random digit to the passphrase.
Number of secrets to emit, one per line. Default 1 (max 100000).
Number of words. Default 6 (~77 bits, max 1024).
Separator between words. Default '-'. Must not contain newlines.
Coloured output: auto (default when omitted), always, or never

exit_codes.description: Success (a closed downstream pipe, e.g. | head -1, also exits 0 — not an error)
Usage error: unknown flag, bad --charset/--encoding value, non-positive or oversized --length/--bytes/--words/--count, unexpected positional (including an unrecognised subcommand)
Runtime error: OS CSPRNG failure or output write failure (disk full, device error)

io.stdout: The generated secret(s), one per line; or a JSON envelope under --json.
io.stderr: Entropy note (≈ N bits) unless --quiet/--json; errors.

composes_with.description: Copy a generated passphrase to the clipboard
